content.cn_text: 你将听到两名犯罪学学生和他们的讲师之间的讨论。第一个
content.en_text: You will hear a discussion between two students of criminology and their lecturer. First

content.cn_text: 你有一些时间阅读问题21-25。[暂停30秒]
content.en_text: you have some time to look at questions 21-25. [Pause 30 seconds]

content.cn_text: 现在仔细听录音，回答问题21-25。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21-25.

content.cn_text: SI:这是一个非常精彩的演讲。
content.en_text: SI: That was a really fascinating lecture.

content.cn_text: S2:有点可怕!
content.en_text: S2: If a bit gruesome!

content.cn_text: L:嗯，1811年的拉特克利夫谋杀案确实对当时的英国社会产生了重大影响。引发公众愤怒和歇斯底里的不仅仅是无辜的当事人被卷入其中或两户人家被残忍杀害的事实。杀人犯或杀人犯——如你所知，他们可能有一个共犯——侵入受害者神圣的家中，犯下真正可怕的罪行。
content.en_text: L: Well, the Ratcliffe murders of 1811 certainly had a significant impact on English society at the time. It wasn't just the fact that innocent parties were involved or that two households were brutally murdered that provoked public outrage and hysteria. The murderer or murderers - since there may, as you know, have been an accomplice -invaded the sanctity of their victims' homes to commit truly horrendous crimes.

content.cn_text: SI:英国人的家就是他的城堡，对吗?所以我猜在那之后，没有人在自己舒适的家里感到真正的安全?
content.en_text: SI: An Englishman's home is his castle, right? So I guess no one felt truly safe in the comfort of their own home after that?

content.cn_text: L:没错。这也是为什么入室行窃也会对受害者产生如此深远的影响。问题22:通常不是贵重物品或职位的丢失;甚至是最让受害者不安的不便，但事实是，他们在自己家里安全的信念受到了严重损害。
content.en_text: L: Exactly. That's also why burglaries, too have such a profound effect on their victims. It's often not the loss of valuables or pos; sessions or even the inconvenience that upsets a victim most, but the fact that their conviction of being safe in their own home lias been severely compromised.

content.cn_text: SI:但是公众的歇斯底里也爆发了，不是吗，因为有一种公共安全受到威胁的感觉。人们认为，当时当地的警察力量是不够的。
content.en_text: SI: But public hysteria broke out too, didn't it, because there was a feeling that public safety was under threat. It was felt that the localised police forces at the time were inadequate.

content.cn_text: L:非常正确。当时的警察力量有限。他们可以执行县长的裁决，但他们有一个巨大的劣势，
content.en_text: L: Very true. The police force at the time had limited power. They could enforce the rulings of a local magistrate for the county but they had one huge disadvantage,

content.cn_text: S2:县警察之间没有沟通，是吗?
content.en_text: S2: There was no communication between county police forces, was there?

content.cn_text: 李:是的。在一个县内发生的犯罪细节没有公布。显然，当一个罪犯越过国界时，他们通常可以成功地逃脱追捕。
content.en_text: L: Yes. Details of crimes committed within a county were not shared. Obviously,when a criminal crossed county borders they could often successfully evade capture.

content.cn_text: SI:今天看来这太荒谬了!
content.en_text: SI: Today that seems so absurd!

content.cn_text: L:现在，全国和世界各地的警察都可以通过警察电脑系统即时获取信息，所以确实是这样。但别忘了，直到1829年罗伯特•皮尔爵士(Sir Robert Peel)组建了伦敦大都会警察局(Metropolitan Police Force)，犯罪记录才开始在全国范围内得到整合。即使在那时，伦敦警区以外甚至是伦敦警区之内的一些地区仍然不在伦敦警察部队的管辖范围之内。
content.en_text: L: Nowadays, with information instantly accessible to all police forces nationally and worldwide on police computer systems, yes it does. But don't forget that it was only with the formation of London's Metropolitan Police Force in 1829 by Sir Robert Peel that criminal records could begin to be consolidated on a national basis. Even then, some areas outside and even within the Metropolitan Police District remained beyond the jurisdiction of the Metropolitan Police Force.

content.cn_text: S2:那么，拉特克利夫谋杀案是否给当时打击犯罪的部队敲响了警钟呢?
content.en_text: S2: Were the Ratcliffe murders a bit of a wake-up call, then, to the crime-fighting forces of the time?

content.cn_text: L:不仅如此，他们还促进了伦敦警察厅的形成。公众在促成急需的改革中也发挥了不小的作用
content.en_text: L: More than that, they acted as a catalyst In the formation of the Metropolitan Police. The public, too, played no small part in bringing about the much needed shake-up in the

content.cn_text: 那个时代的警察系统。
content.en_text: policing systems of the era.

content.cn_text: SI:所以大都会警察局是在臭名昭著的开膛手杰克谋杀案发生半个多世纪之前成立的?
content.en_text: SI: So the Metropolitan Force was founded over half a century before the infamous Jack the Ripper murders?

content.cn_text: L:是的，然而，尽管原力尽了最大的努力，开膛手仍然逍遥法外。然而，人们提出了许多嫌疑人，从儿童作家刘易斯·卡罗尔到艺术家沃尔特·西克特。尽管在这次事件中明显未能伸张正义，但伦敦警察厅不能因为警察程序上的任何不足而受到指责。事实上，伦敦警察厅的建立是英国治安的一个转折点。
content.en_text: L: Yes, however, in spite of the Force's best efforts, the Ripper remained at large.Many suspects were put forward though, ranging from children's author Lewis Carroll through to artist Walter Sickert. Despite the notable failure to deliver justice on this occasion, the Metropolitan Police Force could not be blamed for any Inadequacies in police procedure.  In fact the creation of the Metropolitan Police Force was a turning point in English law-keeping.

content.cn_text: 在你们听完剩下的讨论之前你们有些时间看问题26-30。[暂停30秒]
content.en_text: Narrator: Before you hear the rest of the discussion you have some time to look at questions 26-30. [Pause 30 seconds]

content.cn_text: 现在听录音，回答问题26-30。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 26-30.

content.cn_text: SI:所以一旦公众得到了他们想要的——一支有组织的警察部队——他们高兴吗?
content.en_text: SI: So once the public got what they wanted - an organised police force - were they happy?

content.cn_text: L:可以想象，情况就是这样。然而，公众实际上对新执法人员表现出不满。
content.en_text: L: One would Imagine that that would have been the case. However, the public in fact showed a resentment towards the new law enforcers.

content.cn_text: S2:真的吗?
content.en_text: S2: Really?

content.cn_text: L:是的，以至于公众给他们起了贬义的名字，比如“剥皮者”和“博比”——这些绰号来自于伦敦警察厅创始人罗伯特·皮尔爵士的名字。
content.en_text: L: Yes, so much so that the public gave them derogatory names like 'Peelers' and'Bobbies' - nicknames derived from the name of the Metropolitan Force's founder, Sir Robert Peel.

content.cn_text: 我想那时没有多少人愿意为大都会警察局工作吧?
content.en_text: S2:1 guess not many people would have wanted to work for the Metropolitan Police then?

content.cn_text: L:好吧，至少那是一份有报酬的全职工作。事实上，直到亨利·菲尔丁(Henry Fielding)的出现，警察才首次成为一种永久性的全职职业。1749年，他组建了一支用手枪武装的部队，被称为“鲍街跑步者”，由地方长官办公室负责并支付薪水。在此之前，警务工作依赖于为特定教区工作的无薪夜间守夜人和警察。男性户主被选为守望者或警察，轮流或任命工作。守夜人只带了一根手杖，与当时的罪犯相比，他们是一个糟糕的对手，装备同样落后的警察也是如此。那些被任命为守夜人或巡警的人对他们的任务缺乏热情，这并不奇怪，因为他们必须把白天的正常工作结合起来。
content.en_text: L: Well, at least It was full-time paid work. In fact, policing only first became a permanent full-time profession with the advent of Henry Fielding. He formed a force armed with pistols, known as the 'Bow Street Runners' in 1749, answerable to and paid by a magistrate's office. Prior to that, policing had relied on unpaid night watchmen and constables working for a particular parish. Male householders were chosen for the jobs of watchman or constable, working by rotation or appointment.   Armed with no more than a cane, the watchmen were a poor match for the criminals of the day as were the constables who were similarly ill-equipped. Those appointed as watchmen or constables not surprisingly lacked enthusiasm for their task which had to be combined with their normal daytime employment.

content.cn_text: SI:那么，伦敦警察厅在招聘警官时是否有严格的选拔程序?
content.en_text: SI: So, did the Metropolitan Police Force operate a tough selection process for employing officers?

content.cn_text: L:与当代警察部队一样，他们也有一定的身高和健康水平。但当新成员加入部队时，他们不得不这样做
content.en_text: L: There were the same conditions for a certain height and fitness level as In contemporary police forces. But when new recruits entered the Force they had to

content.cn_text: 从底层开始。没有阶级或背景方面的让步。问题30:这在今天仍然适用。
content.en_text: work their wav up from the bottom. No concessions were made for class or background. This still holds true today.

content.cn_text: 旁白:这是第三部分的结尾。你有半分钟的时间检查答案。[暂停30秒]
content.en_text: Narrator: That's the end of Part 3. You have half a minute to check your answers. [Pause 30 seconds]

groups.desc:
Questions 21-25
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

items.title: The Ratcliffe murders were particularly shocking

options.title: A
options.content: since they remained unsolved.

options.title: B
options.content: because they showed failings in the police force.

options.title: C
options.content: as the privacy of the home was violated.

options.title: D
options.content: since the victims' homes were also burgled.

items.analysis: Ratcliffe谋杀案之所以特别令人震惊，是因为凶手侵犯了受害者家庭的隐私和神圣性。文中明确指出凶手"invaded the sanctity of their victims' homes"（侵入了受害者家庭的神圣领域），这直接对应了选项C中的"privacy of the home was violated"（家庭隐私被侵犯）。这种对家庭隐私的侵犯比单纯的谋杀或盗窃更令人恐惧，因为它动摇了人们对家庭作为安全避风港的基本信念。

items.title: Victims of burglaries are particularly

options.title: A
options.content: fearful of not being safe in their own house.

options.title: B
options.content: traumatised by theft of personal items.

options.title: C
options.content: upset by the inconvenience.

options.title: D
options.content: annoyed with themselves at not having properly secured their homes.

items.analysis: 文中明确指出，入室盗窃的受害者最感到困扰的是他们在自己家中安全的信念被严重损害。这直接对应了选项A中提到的"担心在自己家里不安全"。虽然原文也提到了财物损失和不便，但强调这些并不是最主要的影响。因此，选项A最准确地反映了原文的核心意思。

items.title: Before the formation of the Metropolitan Police Force

options.title: A
options.content: criminal records did not exist.

options.title: B
options.content: most criminal records were inaccurate.

options.title: C
options.content: local police officers held no power.

options.title: D
options.content: policing was not centralised.

items.analysis: 文中提到在大都会警察部队成立之前，各县之间不共享犯罪信息，罪犯能够通过跨越县界逃脱追捕。这明确表明当时的警务系统是分散的，而非中央集权的。因此，选项D（policing was not centralised）最准确地描述了Metropolitan Police Force成立之前的警务状况。

items.title: Both the Ratcliffe and Ripper murders

options.title: A
options.content: remain unsolved.

options.title: B
options.content: were probably committed by more than one person.

options.title: C
options.content: exposed faults in policing systems.

options.title: D
options.content: happened in the eighteenth century.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A（remain unsolved），因为文中对于Ratcliffe案件和Ripper案件都暗示或直接表明案件未被解决。对于Ratcliffe案件，使用"murderer or murderers"的表述显示案件细节不明确，暗示案件可能未解决。而对于Ripper案件，"remained at large"明确表示罪犯未被捕获，即案件未解决。因此，两个案件的共同点是都未被解决，符合A选项。

items.title: When the Metropolitan Police Force was first formed

options.title: A
options.content: all UK police forces had to answer to it.

options.title: B
options.content: the public was disapproving.

options.title: C
options.content: a new era of policing started.

options.title: D
options.content: its faults were immediately apparent.

items.analysis: 文中明确指出伦敦警察厅的创立是英国执法史上的一个转折点（turning point）。这意味着它标志着一个新时代的开始，因为"turning point"通常表示一个重大的变化或新阶段的开始。这与选项C "a new era of policing started"（警务工作开始了一个新时代）直接对应，因此C是正确答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 26-30
What does the lecturer say about the following?
Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to questions 26-30.

items.title: The Bow Street Runners
items.analysis: 文中明确提到Bow Street Runners是"answerable to and paid by a magistrate's office"（对地方法官办公室负责并由其支付薪酬）。地方法官办公室属于地方政府机构，因此B选项"paid by local government"（由地方政府支付）是正确的。这表明Bow Street Runners是一支由地方政府资助的警察力量，而不是私人或志愿组织。

items.title: Night Watchmen
items.analysis: 文中明确指出守夜人"Armed with no more than a cane"（仅配备一根手杖），这表明他们没有配备枪支。手杖显然不属于firearms（枪支）的范畴。此外，"ill-equipped"（装备不足）这一描述进一步强调了守夜人缺乏有效武器的事实。因此，选项A "not in possession of firearms"（没有配备枪支）是正确答案。

items.title: Constables
items.analysis: 根据原文描述，巡警（constables）和警卫（watchmen）只配备了手杖（cane）作为武器，这表明他们没有配备火器。文中明确指出他们装备不足（ill-equipped），这进一步支持了他们没有更先进武器的结论。因此，选项A "not in possession of firearms"（没有配备火器）是正确的。

items.title: The Metropolitan Police Force
items.analysis: 文中明确指出，无论新招募的警察的阶级或背景如何，都必须从底层做起，不会有任何特殊待遇。这表明Metropolitan Police Force在招募和晋升方面采取了无歧视的政策。"No concessions were made for class or background"这句话直接表明了Metropolitan Police Force不会因为个人的阶级或背景而给予特殊待遇，这正是"non-discriminatory"（无歧视）的体现。因此，选项C（non-discriminatory）是正确答案。

items.title: Contemporary Police Forces
items.analysis: 文中明确指出，无论新招募的警员来自什么阶级或背景，他们都必须从底层做起，不会因为背景而得到特殊待遇。这一政策一直延续到今天的警察部队。这表明现代警察部队在招募和晋升方面是非歧视性的，不会因为个人的社会背景而给予特殊对待。因此，选项C（non-discriminatory）最符合这一描述。
